episodes.title: This Cult Leader’s Skin Turned BLUE
episodes.description:
In Part 2 of the Love Has Won story, the cult spirals into chaos as Amy Carlson's health rapidly deteriorates under years of alcohol abuse, starvation, and colloidal silver. As "Mother God" nears death, her followers refuse medical care, convinced she's ascending—leading to one of the most disturbing endings in cult history.

episodes.title: The Rise Of The Love Has Won Cult
episodes.description:
Amy Carlson claimed she was God. Her followers believed dead celebrities were guiding her, abandoned their families to join her commune, and devoted their lives to "Mother God" In Part 1 of Crimes Of: Cults, we trace the shocking rise of Love Has Won, exploring how an ordinary mother became one of the most infamous cult leaders in modern history, and how her bizarre beliefs drew in followers from around the world.

episodes.title: Inside the Final Days of the Solar Temple Cult | Order of the Solar Temple: Part 2
episodes.description:
His own son exposed him as a liar. The rituals were fake. The cosmic messages... nothing but smoke and mirrors. The Order of the Solar Temple should have fallen apart.


But Joseph Di Mambro wouldn't let that happen. If he wasn't going to get what he wanted... nobody could.


In Part 2, Corinne and Sabrina reveal how the collapse of OTS led to three waves of death across three years. When Joseph's illusions were exposed in 1990, he doubled down... telling followers the apocalypse was imminent and only the faithful would survive. On October 5th, 1994, fires erupted simultaneously in Switzerland and Quebec. 53 people dead, including 8 children. But the nightmare didn't end there.


Over the next three years, two more waves of deaths followed. Former Olympic skiers. Policemen. A psychotherapist who claimed she'd made contact with Joseph from beyond. The final toll: 74 people dead across three countries. Eleven of them children.


This is the devastating conclusion to the Order of the Solar Temple... and a warning about what happens when belief becomes weaponized.
If you’re new here, don’t forget to follow Crimes Of… to never miss a case! For Ad-free listening and early access to episodes, subscribe to Crime House+ on Apple Podcasts. Crimes Of… is a Crime House Original Podcast, powered by PAVE Studios

episodes.title: They Built A Religion That Killed 74 People | Order of the Solar Temple: Part 1
episodes.description:
A man obsessed with mysticism. A charismatic life coach seeking deeper meaning. Two paths that should never have crossed... but when they did, they built a religion that killed 74 people.

Step inside the mirrored chamber. Feel the weight of the white cape with the red cross settle on your shoulders. Hear the man in gold tell you that you've been chosen, that a UFO is coming to carry you to the stars. This is the moment the Order of the Solar Temple was selling.

In Part 1, Corinne and Sabrina unravel how Joseph Di Mambro and Luc Jouret created one of the most elaborate and deadly cults in modern history. With 442 members across four countries, they promised salvation through medieval rituals, past-life revelations, and a journey to the planet Sirius. But behind the spectacle was a twisted system of "cosmic marriages," financial exploitation, and a so-called divine infant born to herald the apocalypse.

When Joseph's own son discovers the projectors, trick mirrors, and hidden wires, the foundation begins to crack, setting the stage for Part 2's devastating conclusion.

episodes.title: The Vampire Cult: A Real Cult That Ended in Murder
episodes.description:
They drank each other’s blood. They gave themselves vampire names. They believed they were becoming something immortal.

But the real horror of the Vampire Clan wasn’t fantasy... it was murder.

In this episode, Sabrina and Corinne unravel the disturbing case of Rod Ferrell, the self-styled teen vampire who built a following of vulnerable outsiders and led them down a path of manipulation, fear, and brutal violence.

episodes.title: How a Cult Took Over an Oregon Town
episodes.description:
What started as a spiritual utopia in the Oregon desert became one of the most disturbing cult stories in American history.

In this episode, Sabrina and Corinne dive into the Rajneesh Movement; from Bhagwan Shree Rajneesh’s rise as a global guru to Ma Anand Sheela’s ruthless grip on power inside Rajneeshpuram. What followed was paranoia, political manipulation, mass poisoning, and the largest bioterror attack in U.S. history.

How did a commune built on enlightenment spiral into crime, control, and chaos?
